data.title: Virtune offers commission-free buying for selected crypto ETPs like Bitcoin, XRP, and Solana on Finanzen.net Zero, facilitating monthly savings plans for investors to invest long-term in crypto assets without paying any fees.
data.description: On September 19, 2025, in Frankfurt, Virtune revealed that several crypto ETPs, including Virtune Coinbase 50 Index ETP, Bitcoin, XRP, and Solana, can now be purchased without commission fees on the Finanzen.net Zero platform. This move aims to simplify the process for investors looking to establish a recurring, commission-free monthly savings plan in crypto assets, particularly Bitcoin. By enabling investors to set up monthly savings plans, Virtune allows for automatic, long-term investments in their ETPs on a commission-free basis. This service is now accessible for Virtune's crypto ETPs through Finanzen.net Zero, presenting investors with the opportunity to invest in a hassle-free manner. The benefits of utilizing monthly savings plans and investing in Virtune's ETPs are now conveniently accessible through Finanzen.net Zero.

data.title: IEM & Simon launch new retail platform for high-growth brands, redefining mall experience. - Philadelphia, Sept. 19, 2025
data.description: A recent development in the retail industry is reshaping in-mall retail by introducing all-inclusive experiential retail spaces for top consumer brands. This venture, led by IEM and supported by Simon®, aims to provide a unique shopping experience for customers. The initiative, based in Philadelphia and announced on Sept. 19, 2025, demonstrates the changing landscape of brick-and-mortar retail. By offering turnkey solutions, this partnership is set to revolutionize traditional retail concepts. Stay tuned for the latest updates on this collaboration, as it unfolds to benefit both consumers and participating brands.

data.title: Stock futures are down, but MoneyHero Ltd. expects to report a loss of 2 cents per share on revenue of $19.92 million. FedEx posted strong Q1 results.
data.description: U.S. stock futures are trading lower this morning, with investor focus expected to be on certain stocks. MoneyHero Ltd. (NASDAQ: MNY) is anticipated to report a quarterly loss of 2 cents per share with revenue reaching $19.92 million before the opening bell. In after-hours trading, MoneyHero shares rose by 8.1% to $2.40. On the other hand, FedEx Corp. (NYSE: FDX) revealed better-than-expected financial results for the first quarter of fiscal 2026 on Thursday. For more details, the complete story can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Worthington Enterprises (NYSE: WOR) prepares for Q1 print, expecting to report earnings of 70 cents/share and revenue of $291.84 million, up from the year-ago period. Stocks rose by 2.8% to close at $62.75.
data.description: Worthington Enterprises, Inc. (NYSE: WOR) is set to announce its first-quarter earnings on Tuesday, Sept. 23, after the market closes. Analysts predict the Columbus, Ohio-headquartered company will post earnings of 70 cents per share, an increase from 50 cents per share in the same quarter last year. The company anticipates quarterly revenue to reach $291.84 million, up from $257.31 million in the previous year. Worthington Enterprises recently exceeded expectations with its fourth-quarter financial results. Following the news, the company's shares rose by 2.8% to $62.75 on Thursday. Those interested can find the complete story on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Arbitrum (ARB) stays at $0.52 post 92.65M token unlock; bulls aim for $0.58 resistance, technicals hint at rally.
data.description: Following the unlock of 92.65 million tokens, the price of ARB has managed to hold steady at the $0.52 support level. Technical analysis indicates a possible uptrend towards the $0.58 resistance area.
